errors.core-data-required: Import Core Book Data in the Lancer Compendium Manager (Compendiums tab) before importing a pilot.
errors.unrecognized-format: Could not determine COMP/CON import format for this pilot data.
errors.not-pilot: COMP/CON import target is not a pilot actor.
errors.missing-fields: COMP/CON import data is missing required pilot fields (name/callsign).
errors.invalid-json: Selected file is not valid JSON.

result.title: Pilot Import Result
result.partial: Partially imported {name}. Some actors or items could not be found.
result.missing-actors: Actors that could not be created or updated
result.lcp-hint: Install required LCPs using the Lancer Compendium Manager, then re-import.

ref-slot.empty-hint: Drag an item here or click to browse compendiums
ref-slot.picker-empty: No matching compendium items found. Import the required LCP first.

header.inactive-warning: Warning: Inactive Mech - Pilot bonuses not tracked

abilities.no-pilot: No pilot is assigned to this mech. Assign a pilot on the Combat tab to view talents and frame bonuses.

loadout.reset-mount-confirm: Clear all weapons and mods from this mount?
loadout.reset-all-mounts-confirm: Reset every weapon mount to frame defaults? This cannot be undone.
loadout.reset-systems-confirm: Remove all mounted systems from this mech?

loadout-editor.title: Loadout overview
loadout-editor.hint: Summary of weapon mounts, systems, and SP usage. Use the editor below to drag items and manage mounts.

cloud-header.label: RM-4://(OMNINET UPLINK ID)
cloud-header.vaultID: Load from your Comp/Con account (if logged in)

sync-banner.message: This pilot has not been imported from COMP/CON yet. Open the Sync tab to download or upload a JSON export.

cloud-wizard.logged-in: Signed in — {count} pilots available in the vault list.
cloud-wizard.logged-out: Optional: sign in to list pilots from your COMP/CON vault. Share codes work without login.
cloud-wizard.json-hint: Upload a pilot JSON exported from COMP/CON.
cloud-wizard.tour-missing: Pilot import tour is not registered.

stat-tooltip.hp: Hit Points — damage you can take before being downed out of your mech.
stat-tooltip.overshield: Overshield — temporary HP from effects; lost first when you take damage.
stat-tooltip.armor: Armor — reduces incoming pilot-scale damage by this amount.
stat-tooltip.evasion: Evasion — difficulty to hit you with attacks that target Evasion.
stat-tooltip.edef: Electronic Defense — difficulty to hit you with tech attacks and electronic effects.
stat-tooltip.speed: Speed — how many spaces you move per move action on foot.
stat-tooltip.save: Save — bonus to your pilot saves.
stat-tooltip.sensor_range: Sensor Range — how far you can reliably detect and target with pilot-scale sensors.
stat-tooltip.license_level: License Level — highest license rank used to unlock mech gear and frames.
stat-tooltip.grit: Grit — resource spent to fuel grit-powered abilities and rolls.
stat-tooltip.hull: Hull — trigger for bracing, melee, lifting, and physical endurance checks.
stat-tooltip.agi: Agility — trigger for dodging, initiative, acrobatics, and finesse checks.
stat-tooltip.sys: Systems — trigger for hacking, tech skill, and systems-focused checks.
stat-tooltip.eng: Engineering — trigger for repairs, jury-rigging, and technical problem-solving checks.

floatingDamageNumbers.name: Floating Damage Numbers
floatingDamageNumbers.hint: Enable floating damage numbers for changes to stats, including applying damage rolls. This setting is separate for each client.

enabled.name: Automated Animations Integration
enabled.hint: Enable Lancer support for the Automated Animations module when it is installed and active.

damage.name: Automated Animations on Damage Cards
damage.hint: Trigger Automated Animations when Lancer damage cards are posted, in addition to attack cards.

autorecMode.name: Auto-Apply Lancer AA Global Menu
autorecMode.hint: On startup, import the bundled Lancer AutoAnimations Global Recognition menu. Use Merge to keep existing entries, or Overwrite to replace category menus.

uiTheme.name: UI Theme
uiTheme.hint: Colour scheme for the UI, echoing Comp/Con's themes.

shareProxy.name: COMP/CON Share Import Proxy URL
shareProxy.hint: Optional URL for a same-origin proxy endpoint that can perform COMP/CON share-code requests server-side. Leave blank to call COMP/CON directly from browser.

statusIconsConfig.menu-hint: Configure the status icons that appear on the token HUD
statusIconsConfig.defaultConditionsStatus-desc: Enable the default icon set for conditions & status
statusIconsConfig.cancerConditionsStatus-desc: Enable Cancermantis' icon set for conditions & status
statusIconsConfig.cancerNPCTemplates-desc: Enable Cancermantis' icon set for NPC templates
statusIconsConfig.hayleyConditionsStatus-desc: Enable Hayley's icon set for conditions & status
statusIconsConfig.hayleyPC-desc: Enable Hayley's icon set for PC system effects
statusIconsConfig.hayleyNPC-desc: Enable Hayley's icon set for NPC system effects
statusIconsConfig.hayleyUtility-desc: Enable Hayley's icon set for utility indicators
statusIconsConfig.tommyConditionsStatus-desc: Enable Tommy's icon set for conditions & status

context.cloud-import: Import pilots from COMP/CON cloud sync or a JSON export. Use the wizard steps below, or start the guided import tour.
context.automation: These options control automatic hit calculation, structure/stress prompts, template cleanup, and the optional damage prompt after attacks.
context.accdiff: Confirm accuracy, difficulty, targets, and cover here. Plugins and measured templates are under Advanced.

cloud-import.title: COMP/CON import
cloud-import.body: Log into COMP/CON from Configure Settings → LANCER, then use the Cloud tab wizard to select a pilot or paste a share code. JSON exports from COMP/CON can be uploaded on the same tab.

automation.title: Automation settings
automation.body: Attack roll evaluation calculates hits on attack cards. Prompt Damage After Attack (off by default) opens the damage HUD when at least one target is hit. Structure/Stress prompts appear when a mech reaches 0 HP or exceeds heat cap.

accdiff.title: Attack Acc/Diff HUD
accdiff.body: The sliding HUD confirms bonuses and penalties before an attack roll. Select targets on the canvas or with a measured template, then confirm. Use Advanced for weapon plugins and fine per-target modifiers.

automation.menu-hint: Set up what system automation options are enabled, or turn them all off
automation.master-switch-desc: Turn this off to do everything manually
automation.attacks-desc: Toggle for whether or not you want the system to auto-calculate hits and other attack related automation.
automation.damage-desc: Toggle for whether or not you want the system to apply rolled damage to targets.
automation.structure-desc: Automatically prompt for a structure or stress roll when a mech reaches 0 hp or exceeds its heat cap.
automation.overcharge_heat-desc: Automatically apply heat when rolling overcharge
automation.attack_self_heat-desc: When a weapon has the Overkill or Heat (self) tags, automatically apply any heat.
automation.remove_templates-desc: When the turn changes, should measured templates from attacks be cleared?
automation.limited_loading-desc: When items are used, should their limited and loading status be automatically managed and tracked?
automation.npc_recharge-desc: Automatically roll to recharge NPC systems at the start of their turn.
automation.token_size-desc: Automatically force tokens sizes to match the associated actor's size stat.
automation.prompt_damage_after_attack-desc: When an attack hits at least one target, open the damage roll HUD automatically after the attack card is posted.

rememberAdvanced.name: Remember Acc/Diff Advanced Section
rememberAdvanced.hint: When enabled, the expanded/collapsed state of the Advanced section in the attack Acc/Diff HUD is remembered per weapon.

accdiff.showAdvanced: Show plugins, templates, and per-target modifiers

actionTracker.menu-hint: Configure options related to action tracker HUD and automations.
actionTracker.showHotbar-desc: Toggles the display of the floating hotbar during combat.
actionTracker.allowPlayers-desc: Allows players to modify their own actions.
actionTracker.printMessages-desc: Prints chat messages for start and end of a token's turn.
actionTracker.showTextLabels-desc: Show text labels beside action icons on the floating action tracker.

damage.add-target-bonus-damage: Add a bonus damage type for only this target
damage.cannot-be-reduced-tip: For 'cannot be reduced' effects like the Paracausal mod
damage.half-damage-tip: For effects which cause the attacker to deal half damage in addition to resistance, like Heavy Gunner
damage.paracausal-tip: For 'cannot be reduced' effects like the Paracausal mod

structstress.no-roll: No {stat} remaining. No roll is available.

initiative.sorttracker-desc: Moves the unit currently taking their turn to the top of the tracker and moves units that have taken their turn to the end of the tracker.
initiative.appearancesettings-desc: Configure the colors and icon used in the tracker

squaregriddiagonals.name: Square Grid Diagonal Movement
squaregriddiagonals.hint: How distances will be calculated when moving diagonally on a square grid.
squaregriddiagonals.121: 1-2-1: Diagonal moves alternate cost from 1 to 2

description.crushing: Your mech is damaged beyond repair – it is destroyed. You may still exit it as normal.

direct.3plus: Your mech is @LancerID[stunned] until the end of your next turn.
direct.2: Roll a HULL check. On a success, your mech is @LancerID[stunned] until the end of your next turn. On a failure, your mech is destroyed.

description.trauma: Parts of your mech are torn off by the damage. Roll 1d6. On a 1–3, all weapons on one mount of your choice are destroyed; on a 4–6, a system of your choice is destroyed. LIMITED systems and weapons that are out of charges are not valid choices. If there are no valid choices remaining, it becomes the other result. If there are no valid systems or weapons remaining, this result becomes a DIRECT HIT instead.
description.glancing: Emergency systems kick in and stabilize your mech, but it’s @LancerID[impaired] until the end of your next turn.

description.irreversible: The reactor goes critical. your mech suffers a reactor meltdown at the end of your next turn.

meltdown.2: Roll an ENGINEERING check. On a success, your mech is @Compendium[world.status-items.Exposed]. On a failure, it suffers a reactor meltdown after 1d6 of your turns (rolled by the GM). A reactor meltdown can be prevented by retrying the ENGINEERING check as a free action.
meltdown.1: Your mech suffers a reactor meltdown at the end of your next turn.

description.destabilized: The power plant becomes unstable, beginning to eject jets of plasma. Your mech becomes @Compendium[world.status-items.Exposed].
description.shunt: Your mech’s cooling systems manage to contain the increasing heat; however, your mech becomes @Compendium[world.status-items.Impaired] until the end of your next turn.

direct.3plus: The Monstrosity is smacked in the head and is @LancerID[stunned] until the end of its next turn.
direct.2: The Monstrosity must succeed on a HULL save or be destroyed.

description.dismember: The attack blows a limb or chunk off the Monstrosity; it takes 1d6 KINETIC damage and becomes @LancerID[slow] for the rest of the scene.
description.powerful: The Monstrosity is knocked @LancerID[prone] by the force of the blow.
description.glancing: The Monstrosity flinches in pain; it’s @LancerID[impaired] until the end of its next turn.

manual_token_size.label: Manual Token Sizing
manual_token_size.hint: Bypass system automation for determining token size

LANCERINITIATIVE.IconDesc: CSS classes to define the activation icon.
LANCERINITIATIVE.DeactivateIconDesc: CSS classes to define the deactivation icon.
LANCERINITIATIVE.ActivatedLastDesc: Moves units that have taken their turn to the end of the tracker.
LANCERINITIATIVE.SortTrackerDesc: Moves the unit currently taking their turn to the top of the tracker and moves units that have taken their turn to the end of the tracker.
LANCERINITIATIVE.EnableInitiativeDesc: Adds a button to owned combatants for rolling initiative
